declarations.name: HTMLElementBase
declarations.description:
SSR-safe `HTMLElement` base. In the browser it is the real `HTMLElement`; under Node — an SSR
pass, a build step, or the dist smoke check — it is an inert stand-in, so merely *importing*
the package never throws `HTMLElement is not defined`. The elements only do anything once a
browser upgrades them (every `customElements.define` is guarded too), so the stand-in is never
constructed.

declarations.name: cellForce
declarations.description:
The Field Cell's in-frame force math (§25.1) — a deliberately simplified,
centre-of-frame model for demos. NOT the canonical force math (§6); the cell is
a poster engine. `dx,dy` point from the particle TO the frame centre.

`<field-cell force="swirl" color="#2dd4bf">` — an in-frame field surface (§25.1).

A standalone field sized to its container that renders *one* force, with its own
lightweight particle pool, its own pointer interaction, and a lifecycle that pauses
when off-screen. It is **not** the §-engine: it's a lighter "demo/poster" engine,
completely separate from the page `<field-root>` and the core field loop.

- `ResizeObserver` re-fits the canvas (DPR-aware) and rebuilds the pool.
- `IntersectionObserver` gates the rAF loop — paused when off-screen.
- Honours `prefers-reduced-motion`: renders one static frame, no animation.
declarations.name: FieldCell

members.name: boxW
members.description: frame box in CSS pixels (post-DPR transform space).

members.name: cursorX
members.description: cursor in frame-local CSS px, or null when absent.

members.name: force
members.description: the force this cell demonstrates (§25.1).

members.name: color
members.description: dot color.

members.name: count
members.description: particle count; `0` (default) means auto-size to the frame area.

members.name: fit
members.description: Fit the canvas to the element box (DPR-aware) and rebuild the pool.

members.name: buildPool
members.description: Build the particle pool sized to the frame area.

members.name: step
members.description: Advance + render one frame.

attributes.name: color
attributes.description: Accent color (hex) for the cell's particles.

attributes.name: count
attributes.description: Number of particles in the cell's pool.

declarations.summary:
A standalone, in-frame demo field that renders one force with its own
particle pool, in-view-gated. Registered as `<field-cell>`.

members.name: OPTIONS
members.default: [ { key: 'density', attr: 'density', read: (el) => el.density }, { key: 'waves', attr: 'waves', read: (el) => el.waves }, { key: 'depth', attr: 'depth', read: (el) => el.depth }, { key: 'background', attr: 'background', read: (el) => el.background }, { key: 'render', attr: 'render', read: (el) => el.renderMode }, { key: 'overlay', attr: 'overlay', read: (el) => el.overlay }, { key: 'palette', attr: 'palette', read: (el) => el.palette }, { key: 'mass', attr: 'mass', read: (el) => el.mass }, { key: 'attention', attr: 'attention', read: (el) => el.attention }, { key: 'causality', attr: 'causality', read: (el) => el.causality }, { key: 'heatmap', attr: 'heatmap', read: (el) => el.heatmap }, { key: 'dprCap', attr: 'dpr-cap', read: (el) => el.dprCap }, { key: 'gridWarp', attr: 'grid-warp', read: (el) => el.gridWarp }, ]
members.description:
The engine options `<field-root>` forwards to `createBrowserField`, as ONE declarative table —
the single source of truth for the option object built in `start()`, so a new forwarded
`FieldOption` can never be silently dropped from forwarding the way `depth` once was. `accent`
(raw passthrough so a `palette` with no `accent` adopts the palette stop) and
`overlayCanvas`/`feedbackSink` (managed internally) are special-cased in `start()` and absent here.

`observedAttributes` stays an explicit literal below — the Custom-Elements-Manifest analyzer reads
it statically and can't enumerate a computed array — but the `option-attrs-observed` test pins it
to this table (every `attr` here must be observed), so the two lists can't drift apart.

members.name: overlayCanvas
members.description: Field Surfaces: the optional front overlay surface (light-DOM, above content).

members.name: visibilityObserver
members.description: element-level visibility: pages can hide the field (display:none) — skip draw work then.

members.name: platformRuntime
members.description: experimental platform runtime (Phase D); present only when the flag is on.

members.name: platform
members.description:
The live `@fundamental-engine/dom` instance backing this field (Phase D default), or `undefined` on
the legacy path. Read-only introspection for tools like the Inspector — the registries here are
the real running state (measurements, state, feedback bindings, relationships, overlays, lint).
Read with `measure.last()` etc.; don't call `measure.measure()` off the read phase.

members.name: accent
members.description: the travelling accent (§9); defaults to the first palette color.

members.name: density
members.description: particle-count multiplier (§2.5).

members.name: waves
members.description: draw the background Currents (§24).

members.name: renderMode
members.description:
render mode (§20.6); the DEFAULT is `none` (#538) — the signals-only engine: simulate + feed back,
never draw (#297). Set `render="dots"` (or another drawing mode) to get a visible surface.

members.name: background
members.description:
substrate background: `transparent` (present and not `"false"`) clears to transparent so the
underlay composites over light content; default `opaque` paints the near-black substrate.

members.name: overlay
members.description: Field Surfaces: the overlay reading(s) — one mode or a space-separated additive stack. Default `off`.

members.name: palette
members.description: color template name for the travelling accent (§9), or undefined for `ours`.

members.name: attention
members.description: conserved attention (§2.4): present and not `"false"` → one finite strength budget.

members.name: causality
members.description: cross-boundary causality (Concept 4): present and not `"false"` → density spills.

members.name: mass
members.description: first-class mass (§21.3): present and not `"false"` → particle mass ∝ size.

members.name: heatmap
members.description: density heatmap (field-systems H1): present and not `"false"` → the pooling glow underlay.

members.name: dprCap
members.description: `dpr-cap` — backing-store DPR ceiling (#410); undefined (engine default 2) if absent/invalid.

members.name: depth
members.description: `depth` — optional z-volume; undefined (engine default 0, the flat field) if absent/invalid.

members.name: gridWarp
members.description:
`grid-warp` — `grid` overlay distortion multiplier; undefined (engine default 1) if absent/invalid.
`0` is valid (a flat, undistorted lattice).

members.name: scan
members.description: re-scan the document for `[data-body]` bodies after a DOM change.

members.name: rescan
members.description: alias of `scan`.

members.name: setAccent
members.description: recolor the travelling accent (§9).

members.name: setPalette
members.description: swap the accent color template live (§9).

members.name: setFormation
members.description: switch the global formation (§7).

members.name: setAttention
members.description: toggle conserved attention (§2.4) live.

members.name: setCausality
members.description: toggle cross-boundary causality (Concept 4) live.

members.name: setBackground
members.description: switch the substrate background live: `transparent` composites the underlay over light content.

members.name: setHeatmap
members.description: toggle the density heatmap layer (field-systems H1) live.

members.name: setDprCap
members.description: lower/raise the backing-store DPR ceiling at runtime (the dominant fill-rate lever).

members.name: setRender
members.description: switch the underlay render mode (§20.6) live; `none` = signals-only — stop drawing, keep the signals (#297).

members.name: setOverlay
members.description: render field readings on the overlay surface (Field Surfaces — in front of content); one mode or an additive stack.

members.name: threads
members.description: wire glowing connector lines between a set, or clear with null (§10).

members.name: burst
members.description: a discrete one-shot: shove + heat matter near (x, y), optionally tinting it (§11).

members.name: flowTo
members.description: place/move a dynamic flow focus the field bends toward — pulls matter, curves the streamlines.

members.name: clearFlow
members.description: remove the flow focus.

members.name: seed
members.description: bind a data record to each base particle (its `weight` scales mass + size); pick back with `atomAt`.

members.name: addAgent
members.description: add an engine-stepped agent; returns an inert no-op handle if the field hasn't started.

members.name: addBody
members.description: add a programmatic body (no DOM) from a spec; a no-op handle until the field starts.

members.name: addField
members.description: register a named external scalar field channel the engine samples; inert handle until the field starts.

members.name: sampleField
members.description: sample a registered field channel at (x, y); 0 for an unknown channel or before the field starts.

members.name: atomAt
members.description: the seeded record on the nearest particle to (x, y), or null — for hover-to-inspect.

members.name: focusAt
members.description: focus the nearest seeded particle (hold + light it), returning its record — the dwell affordance.

members.name: clearFocus
members.description: release the focused particle.

members.name: particleCount
members.description: live particle-pool size — `store.size` forwarded through the public handle.

members.name: readParticles
members.description:
copy live particle state into `out` (stride 5: x, y, z, heat, size); returns the count written
(0 before the field starts) — the render-agnostic swarm read-out an alternative surface draws.

members.name: readParticleIds
members.description: copy each live particle's stable id into a Uint32Array, parallel to readParticles.

members.name: energy

type.text: { kinetic: number; thermal: number; total: number; count: number }

members.description: kinetic/thermal/total energy snapshot for the current frame.

members.name: sample
members.description:
sample the live field at `(x, y)` — the net force vector a still test particle would feel
(zero before the field starts). The seam external visualizers consume to build field geometry.

members.name: sampleScalar
members.description: sample the smooth density scalar ∈ [0,1] at `(x, y)` (needs `heatmap`); 0 when off/not started.

members.name: sampleGradient
members.description: sample the density gradient ∇ at `(x, y)` — up-density direction (needs `heatmap`); `{0,0}` when off/not started.

members.name: grid
members.description: open a named host-authorable scalar grid (deposit/sample/gradient/decay); a no-op grid until the field starts.

members.name: on
members.description: subscribe to a discrete field event (absorb/release/settle); a no-op unsubscribe until the field starts.

members.name: start
members.description: (re)create the engine on the canvas, reading the current attributes.

declarations.description:
`<field-root>` — the recommended tag for the singleton field. The registry rejects registering one
constructor under two tag names, so this is a thin subclass of FieldField with identical
behaviour, attributes, and body contract.
declarations.name: FieldRoot

declarations.name: syncBodies
declarations.description:
Sync the scan root's body elements into the MeasurementRegistry (D2). New `[data-body]` /
`[data-preset]` / `[data-intent]` / `[data-field-role]` elements are registered for measurement;
disconnected ones are pruned by the read phase. Returns how many were newly registered. Pure given
the sink + root — the selector is core's `bodyElements`, so the platform never drifts from the
legacy scanner. Note (D2): an element that loses its body attribute while staying connected is not
yet unregistered — a later refinement; today the legacy engine remains the source of truth for
rendering, so this is measurement-only and has no observable effect.

declarations.name: registerShadowBody
declarations.description:
Register a shadow-DOM host for measurement from a `register-body` event detail (D4). The host's
custom `getRect` (for closed roots / inner cores) flows straight into MeasurementRegistry's rect
override. Pure given the sink + detail. `syncBodies`' `if (!has)` guard means it won't clobber a
host registered here, so the two discovery paths coexist (the legacy engine still drives the
body's simulation; the platform owns its geometry).

declarations.name: unregisterShadowBody
declarations.description: Unregister a shadow-DOM host from measurement on its `unregister-body` event (D4). Pure.

declarations.name: shouldDiscoverRelationships
declarations.description:
Relationship discovery is heavier than body syncing (it builds graph edges from every native
link), so the runtime re-discovers on a throttle rather than every frame (D5). Pure.

declarations.name: usePlatformRuntime
declarations.description: Enable (or disable) the platform runtime for every `<field-root>` by default (D6: default on).

declarations.name: isPlatformRuntimeDefault
declarations.description: Whether the platform runtime is the current default.

declarations.name: shouldUsePlatformRuntime

type.text: { getAttribute(name: string): string | null; hasAttribute(name: string): boolean }

declarations.description:
Decide whether an element should use the platform runtime: an explicit `experimental-platform`
attribute opts a single element in/out (`="off"` forces the legacy path), otherwise the global
default applies (on since D6). Pure — the element's only branch point.

declarations.name: makeFeedbackSink
declarations.description:
Build a feedback sink (D3) that routes the engine's per-body channels through the platform's
FeedbackRegistry. The eased density value is the engine's own — only the *write* moves — so the
signal is preserved exactly. Writes apply on the platform's write phase (its scheduler tick).

Since #228 the sink contract is the engine's ONLY write path: with no sink configured,
`createField` installs an internal default sink (`core/feedback-sink.ts`) whose direct writes are
byte-identical to the engine's historical behavior. This platform sink replaces that default when
the runtime is on, moving the same channels onto FeedbackRegistry (write-phase batching,
`cssWritesLastFrame()` accounting, governor throttling).

declarations.name: startPlatformRuntime
declarations.description:
Start the platform runtime over a scan root. Thin DOM glue (rAF + viewport size); SSR-safe — with
no `window` it returns a created-but-idle platform. D2+ register bodies/feedback/relationships
here; D1 measures only the root.
